name: Cultivators
ai_rationale: Cultivation is a physical, land‑bound activity with minimal digital output; AI tools (satellite monitoring, pest‑detection apps) only aid decision‑making and cannot replace manual planting, weeding, or harvesting. Low capital and small plot sizes limit automation, keeping exposure modest despite some AI augmentation.

name: Agricultural labourers
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Agricultural labour involves manual planting, harvesting, and field maintenance, tasks that require physical dexterity and real‑time environmental judgment. AI tools are limited to remote monitoring or equipment guidance, which do not replace the labourer’s hands‑on work.

name: Dairy & livestock workers
ai_rationale: Work is hands‑on animal husbandry, requiring physical presence, manual dexterity, and real‑time judgment about animal health, which AI cannot replicate. Limited digital output means low automation potential despite any decision‑support tools.

name: Fishing & aquaculture
ai_rationale: Work is primarily manual, outdoors, and involves physical capture and handling of fish, with minimal digital or data‑centric tasks. AI tools have limited applicability, and infrastructure constraints keep automation low despite any productivity gains.

name: Construction labour (general / unskilled)
ai_rationale: The role is primarily manual, involving physical tasks on site with minimal digital interaction. AI tools can aid planning or safety monitoring, but cannot replace the hands‑on labor required, especially given limited digitization and low wage arbitrage pressure.

name: Civil engineers
ai_rationale: Civil engineering combines on‑site supervision and physical judgment with digital design tools; AI can automate drafting, structural analysis, and project scheduling, boosting productivity, but core field work and context‑specific decisions remain human‑driven. Indian construction relies heavily on manual labor, limiting automation impact, yet design phases are increasingly software‑centric.

name: Architects
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Architects produce primarily digital design deliverables (CAD, BIM) that AI‑driven generative and drafting tools can automate or accelerate, reshaping the role. However, site coordination, client interaction, and creative judgment remain human‑centric, limiting full replacement. In India, the profession is moderately digital with limited export pressure,{​} supporting a high but not maximal exposure.

name: Textile workers (yarn, fabric, weaving)
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: The role involves manual operation of looms, spinning, weaving, and other physical processes with minimal digital output. AI can assist in predictive maintenance or design, but core tasks require dexterity and on‑site labor, limiting automation impact in India’s cost‑advantaged manufacturing context.

name: Machine operators
ai_rationale: Machine operators perform hands‑on, physical tasks on manufacturing equipment with limited digital output. AI can aid scheduling or predictive maintenance, but robotics adoption is constrained by capital costs and cheap labor in India, keeping core work largely manual.

name: Auto & components
ai_rationale: The role is largely physical—assembly, machining, and inspection on the shop floor—with limited digital output. AI can aid tooling, predictive maintenance, and quality‑control vision systems, but core manual dexterity and real‑time decision‑making remain human‑centric, keeping exposure modest.

name: Quality control inspectors
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: QC inspection relies on physical, visual and tactile assessment of products, limiting digital exposure, but AI-powered vision and sensor systems can automate portions of defect detection, reshaping parts of the role. Indian manufacturing’s capital constraints slow full automation, keeping the job partly viable.

name: Industrial engineers
ai_rationale: Industrial engineers work largely with digital tools—process simulation, data analytics, and optimization software—making their tasks highly amenable to AI augmentation and productivity gains. AI can automate many design and scheduling functions, reshaping the role, though human judgment on plant layout and change management remains essential. Indian manufacturing’s export focus amplifies exposure as AI narrows the cost advantage of low‑skill engineering labor.

name: Retail sales associates
ai_rationale: Retail sales associates rely on in‑person customer interaction and physical product handling; AI can assist with inventory, pricing, and recommendation tools but cannot replace the on‑floor service. The role’s low digital work product and limited automation potential keep exposure modest despite some productivity gains.

name: Wholesale traders
ai_rationale: Wholesale trading relies on physical goods handling, relationship‑based negotiation and offline market intelligence; AI can aid pricing analytics and inventory management but cannot replace the on‑site sourcing and trust functions. The work is largely non‑digital, so exposure is limited to assistance rather than fundamental reshaping.

name: Cashiers
ai_rationale: Cashier work is largely digital—scanning items, processing payments, and managing POS data—tasks that AI‑driven self‑checkout and automated payment systems can perform today. In India, the export‑oriented retail back‑office is limited, but the declining hiring trend and growing adoption of digital checkout in larger stores raise exposure to a high level.

name: Customer service (retail)
ai_rationale: Retail customer service is primarily digital, handling queries via chat, email, and phone, which LLM‑driven chatbots and voice assistants can already perform at near‑human quality. India's large export‑oriented call‑center industry amplifies exposure, and declining hiring reflects AI‑driven productivity gains. Human agents remain for complex or escalated issues, keeping the role partially defensible.

name: TCS engineers
ai_rationale: TCS engineers perform primarily digital software development tasks—coding, testing, and configuration—that AI coding assistants can already execute with high accuracy, dramatically boosting productivity and reducing required headcount. The export‑oriented nature of Indian IT services amplifies exposure as offshore cost advantages erode. While some complex design and client‑interaction elements remain, the core work is highly automatable.
source: TCS Q4 FY25 + Jul 2025 layoff announcement

name: Infosys engineers
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Infosys engineers produce primarily digital code, test scripts, and AI/agent platforms, making their core tasks highly automatable by LLMs and code‑generation tools. AI productivity gains erode the offshore cost advantage, exposing mid‑level engineers to large displacement, while senior architects retain some protection.

name: Wipro engineers
ai_rationale: Wipro engineers produce largely digital deliverables—software code, system designs, and client‑facing solutions—making them highly susceptible to AI‑driven code generation and productivity gains. In India, offshore cost arbitrage amplifies exposure as AI tools compress the value gap with higher‑cost onshore developers. While human oversight remains essential, AI reshapes the role substantially.

name: HCLTech engineers
ai_rationale: Software engineering is a fully digital knowledge work; LLMs already generate code, test, and debug, dramatically boosting productivity and eroding offshore cost advantages. In India's export‑oriented IT services, AI tools can replace a sizable share of junior engineering effort, leading to high exposure despite current flat hiring.

name: Tech Mahindra engineers
ai_rationale: Tech Mahindra engineers perform primarily digital software development and support tasks that AI/LLM tools can generate, test, and maintain, dramatically boosting productivity and eroding offshore cost advantages. While human oversight remains, core coding and routine engineering work is already being automated at scale, placing the role in the high‑exposure bracket.

name: Mid-tier IT services
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Mid-tier IT services involve largely digital tasks—software maintenance, testing, integration, and client support—that AI/LLMs can already automate or dramatically accelerate, especially in export‑oriented Indian BPM and KPO models. High wage arbitrage and modest hiring decline signal strong productivity displacement, even if the role persists.

name: Indian product startups
ai_rationale: The role is fully digital—coding, product design, data analysis, and online collaboration—all of which AI/LLMs can already perform at or near human levels, dramatically boosting productivity. In India, the large offshore software base amplifies wage‑arbitrage pressure, making AI-driven efficiency gains especially impactful.

name: Freelance developers
ai_rationale: Work is fully digital—coding, debugging, and delivering software—all tasks that LLMs and code‑generation tools can already perform at a competent level, dramatically boosting productivity and reducing offshore cost advantage. While complex architecture and client interaction still need humans, AI can replace much of the routine development work, placing the role in the high‑exposure bracket.

name: GCC software engineering
ai_rationale: Software engineering is a fully digital knowledge work where LLMs already generate code, debug and test, dramatically boosting productivity and eroding offshore cost advantages. In India’s export‑oriented GCC model, AI tools can cut required headcount despite continued hiring growth, placing the role at high exposure.

name: GCC engineering R&D
ai_rationale: The role is fully digital, involving software/hardware design, coding, and analysis, which AI/LLMs can already generate, test, and document. In India’s export‑oriented GCC model, AI‑driven productivity sharply reduces the cost advantage of offshore engineers, raising exposure. While some deep R&D insight remains human‑centric, core engineering tasks are highly automatable.

name: GCC data & analytics
ai_rationale: The role is fully digital, centered on data processing, reporting, and statistical analysis, tasks that LLMs and automated analytics platforms already perform at or above junior analyst levels. In India's export‑oriented GCC sector, AI productivity gains can dramatically reduce demand for human analysts, though senior strategic work remains less replaceable.

name: GCC shared services
ai_rationale: GCC shared services are largely digital back‑office functions (finance, HR, IT support, analytics) that can be automated or dramatically accelerated by LLMs and workflow AI. High export orientation and relatively low wages amplify AI’s impact, even though some regulatory or client‑specific safeguards may linger.

name: GCC AI/ML specialists
ai_rationale: The role is fully digital—building, training, and deploying AI models—so AI tools can dramatically augment productivity and reduce headcount needs. However, deep expertise, research, and bespoke model engineering remain human‑centric, limiting full replacement within five years.
source: Zinnov 2025 - 120K+ AI pros in Indian GCCs

name: Voice / call centre agents
hiring_trend_label: Strongly declining (>10% YoY drop in headcount)
ai_rationale: Voice agents perform scripted, digital interactions that LLM‑powered voice bots already replicate at scale, driving a steep productivity multiplier and eroding the offshore cost advantage. The rapid decline in hiring and low wages amplify exposure, though some legacy contracts and regulatory data‑privacy rules provide limited protection.
source: TeamLease via Reuters Oct 2025; LimeChat case

name: Data entry / processing
hiring_trend_label: Strongly declining (>10% YoY drop in headcount)
ai_rationale: The work is entirely digital—reading scanned documents and typing data into systems—making it a prime target for document‑understanding AI, which already matches or exceeds human accuracy. AI can automate 90%+ of the tasks, leaving only minimal human review, and the low wage amplifies offshoring pressure. Regulatory or informal protections are minimal for BPM data‑entry roles.

name: F&A outsourcing
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: F&A outsourcing is a fully digital, back‑office knowledge task (accounting, reporting, reconciliations) that AI/LLM tools can already perform with high accuracy, and the sector is heavily export‑oriented, making wage arbitrage sensitive to AI productivity gains. While some judgment and client interaction remain, the core work is highly automatable.

name: Transaction processing
ai_rationale: Transaction processing is a fully digital, rule‑based BPM task that AI/LLM‑driven workflow automation can already perform at or above human speed, especially for export‑oriented back‑office services. The declining hiring trend and low wage further increase exposure, despite any regulatory or informal protections.

name: Medical transcription / coding
hiring_trend_label: Strongly declining (>10% YoY drop in headcount)
ai_rationale: Medical transcription and coding are fully digital tasks that LLMs and specialized AI (e.g., speech‑to‑text, coding algorithms) already perform at or above human levels, driving a sharp decline in headcount. India's large export‑oriented BPM base amplifies exposure, despite any regulatory or informal protections.

name: HR outsourcing
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: HR outsourcing work is largely screen‑based (candidate screening, payroll, benefits administration, data entry) and aligns with tasks that LLMs and workflow automation already perform at scale. In India, the sector is export‑oriented and wage‑arbitrage sensitive, so AI productivity gains quickly erode demand. While some judgment and client interaction remain, the core workflow is highly automatable.

name: Insurance agents
ai_rationale: Insurance agents rely on personal trust, relationship building, and in‑person sales, which are hard for AI to replace. AI can augment lead generation, policy recommendation, and paperwork automation, but the core role remains physical and interpersonal, limiting exposure to a low‑moderate level.

name: Bank clerks & tellers
ai_rationale: Core tasks—transaction processing, data entry, routine inquiries—are highly digital and can be automated with RPA and conversational AI, reducing demand for clerks. However, face‑to‑face customer service and discretionary judgment in exception handling remain human‑centric, limiting exposure to the highest tier.

name: Accountants & auditors
ai_rationale: Accounting and audit work is largely digital—data entry, financial analysis, report generation, and compliance checks can be largely automated or accelerated by AI tools, especially in India's export‑oriented finance services. While professional judgment and client interaction remain, AI-driven productivity gains can sharply reduce demand for junior accountants, placing the role in the high‑exposure bracket.

name: Financial advisors
ai_rationale: Advisors rely on digital data analysis, report generation, and regulatory compliance, all of which AI can automate or augment, reducing the number of advisors needed for the same output. However, client relationship management, trust, and nuanced judgment remain human‑centric, limiting full replacement.

name: Loan processors / officers
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Loan processing is largely digital—reviewing applications, verifying documents, running credit checks, and generating decisions—all tasks that LLMs and AI underwriting tools can perform today. In India, many of these roles are export‑oriented back‑office functions, making them highly vulnerable to AI‑driven productivity gains despite some regulatory safeguards.

name: NBFC / fintech ops
ai_rationale: Fintech operations are primarily digital back‑office tasks—transaction processing, KYC verification, compliance reporting, and data reconciliation—that AI/LLMs can already automate at scale. The role is export‑oriented and wage‑arbitrage sensitive, making it highly vulnerable to AI‑driven productivity gains. Regulatory protections may slow adoption, but the underlying task exposure remains high.

name: Chartered Accountants
ai_rationale: Chartered accountants work primarily with digital financial data, preparing reports, tax filings, and audits that AI tools can already automate or augment at scale. In India, many CA services are export‑oriented BPM tasks, making them vulnerable to productivity gains from LLM‑driven analysis and document generation. While judgment and client advisory retain a human element, the core routine work is highly digitized and AI‑replaceable.

name: Bank branch managers
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Branch managers oversee both digital operations (reporting, compliance, sales analytics) that AI can augment and in‑person customer relationship and staff management that require human judgment. AI can streamline many back‑office tasks and reduce managerial bandwidth, but the role’s hybrid physical‑digital nature limits full automation.

name: Investment analysts
ai_rationale: Investment analysts work primarily with digital data, financial models, and report writing, all of which are increasingly automatable by LLMs and predictive analytics. AI can boost productivity and reduce headcount needs, especially for offshore analysts, but nuanced judgment, client interaction, and regulatory compliance retain a human element.

name: School teachers (govt)
ai_rationale: Teaching relies heavily on in‑person interaction and judgment, limiting full automation, but AI can automate grading, lesson‑plan generation, and content delivery, reshaping parts of the role. The job’s mixed physical/cognitive nature places it in the mid‑range exposure.

name: Government clerical & admin staff
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Government clerical work involves routine data entry, form processing and record keeping that AI/LLM tools can automate, but large bureaucratic inertia, data security concerns and low digital penetration limit rapid displacement. The role is partially digital, so AI will reshape parts of the job but not replace it entirely in the near term.

name: Police
ai_rationale: Police work is heavily physical, requires real‑time judgment, and unpredictable human interaction; AI can aid with analytics, surveillance, and decision support but cannot replace core duties. Hence exposure is limited to assistance tools, placing it in the low‑range bracket.

name: Other PSU employees
ai_rationale: Most tasks involve bureaucratic administration, paperwork, and routine reporting in public sector undertakings, which can be partially digitized and assisted by AI (e.g., document processing, data entry). However, the work remains tied to regulatory compliance, stakeholder interaction, and on‑site oversight, limiting full digital automation. Indian public‑sector inertia and limited AI adoption further temper exposure.

name: Healthcare staff (govt) â€” doctors, nurses, paramedics
ai_rationale: The role combines physical patient care with administrative tasks; AI can automate record‑keeping, scheduling, and basic decision support, reshaping parts of the job, but core clinical interactions remain human‑centric. Government health services have limited outsourcing and slower tech adoption, moderating overall exposure.

name: Defence personnel
ai_rationale: Defence roles involve physical presence, tactical decision‑making, and secure, non‑digital operations, limiting AI's direct automation. AI can assist with data analysis and simulations, but core duties remain hands‑on and security‑sensitive, keeping exposure low.

name: Indian Railways staff
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Most Railways staff perform physical, on‑site duties (train operation, station management, track maintenance) with limited digital work; AI can assist ticketing and scheduling but cannot replace the core manual and safety‑critical tasks. Hence exposure is low, around the lower end of the 2‑3 band.

name: Postal workers
ai_rationale: Core tasks involve physical mail handling and delivery, which require manual dexterity and in‑person interaction. AI can aid sorting and routing, but the bulk of work remains non‑digital and labor‑intensive, limiting automation potential in India’s current infrastructure.

name: Nurses
ai_rationale: Nursing is primarily physical patient care, requiring dexterity, real‑time judgment and empathy; AI can assist with documentation, scheduling and decision support but cannot replace bedside tasks. The role’s low digital product and high human interaction keep exposure modest despite growing tech aids.

name: Doctors (general practitioners, physicians)
ai_rationale: Doctors combine physical examination, patient interaction, and complex clinical judgment, limiting full digital automation. AI can augment diagnostics, imaging analysis, and documentation, reshaping parts of the workflow, but core decision‑making and bedside care remain human‑centric. Indian private healthcare’s growth and regulatory safeguards keep the role largely intact.

name: Pharmacists
ai_rationale: Pharmacy work blends physical dispensing with digital prescription processing and drug‑interaction checks, tasks that AI can automate or augment substantially. AI‑driven verification and inventory systems can boost productivity, potentially reducing headcount, but human counseling and regulatory judgment remain essential, keeping exposure at a high‑mid level.

name: Hospital admin
ai_rationale: Hospital administrators handle patient scheduling, billing, records management, and regulatory compliance, tasks that are largely digital and increasingly automated by AI‑driven health‑IT systems. In India, cost‑sensitive private hospitals adopt AI for workflow optimization, threatening the demand for human admins despite current flat hiring. The role is therefore highly exposed to AI augmentation and potential displacement.

name: Lab technicians
ai_rationale: Lab technicians perform physical sample preparation and instrument operation, but much of the analytical interpretation can be automated with AI image analysis and diagnostic algorithms. The role is partially digital, so AI can boost productivity and replace some analysis tasks, yet hands‑on work remains essential, limiting exposure to the mid‑range.

name: Diagnostic radiologists
ai_rationale: Radiologists work with fully digital imaging data; AI algorithms already match or exceed human performance on many detection tasks, dramatically augmenting productivity and potentially reducing demand. While final clinical judgment and patient interaction remain human, the core interpretive work is highly automatable, especially in India's export‑oriented diagnostic services.

name: Private tutors
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Tutoring is largely a knowledge‑transfer role that can be digitized; AI‑driven tutoring platforms can automate lesson delivery and basic query handling, reducing demand for human tutors. However, personalized mentorship, classroom dynamics, and trust‑based relationships remain human‑centric, limiting full replacement in the near term.

name: College & university faculty
ai_rationale: Faculty work is largely knowledge‑based and increasingly digital (lecture slides, grading software, research literature searches), so AI can boost productivity and automate parts of assessment and content creation. However, core teaching, mentorship, and original research require human judgment and in‑person interaction, limiting full replacement in the next five years.

name: Coaching / test prep
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Coaching relies on personal interaction and adaptive teaching, limiting full automation, but AI can generate test material, provide instant feedback, and augment tutoring, reshaping a sizable portion of the work. India's large private education export market amplifies exposure, though regulatory and cultural preferences for human teachers temper it.

name: EdTech roles
ai_rationale: EdTech work is largely digitalâcontent creation, curriculum design, platform management, dataâdriven analytics and online instructionâall of which LLMs and generative AI can produce, edit and personalize at scale. In India, many of these roles serve exportâoriented SaaS and Kâ12 platforms, making them highly vulnerable to AIâdriven productivity gains despite current flat hiring.

name: Influencers & creators
ai_rationale: The work is fully digital—creating, editing, and distributing video, audio, and written content—tasks that generative AI can already perform at scale, reducing the need for human creators. AI-driven tools (e.g., LLMs, image/video generators) can produce comparable outputs, making the role highly susceptible to automation despite current hiring booms.

name: Content writers
hiring_trend_label: Strongly declining (>10% YoY drop in headcount)
ai_rationale: Content writing is a fully digital output that LLMs already generate at or above median quality, dramatically lowering unit cost and prompting a sharp hiring decline. Indian writers compete globally on price, so AI productivity erodes the wage arbitrage that sustained the role, leading to very high exposure.

name: Graphic designers
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Design work is fully digital and generative AI can create layouts, illustrations, and branding assets at or above junior designer quality, dramatically boosting productivity and reducing demand for low‑skill designers. Indian firms rely on cost‑effective design outsourcing, so AI erodes the wage arbitrage that sustains the role.

name: Social media managers
ai_rationale: Social media managers produce and curate digital content, analyze engagement metrics, and schedule posts—all tasks that LLMs and AI marketing platforms can automate or augment at scale. In India, the role is largely export‑oriented and low‑wage, making AI‑driven productivity gains especially impactful, though human brand voice and crisis handling still require oversight.

name: Photographers
ai_rationale: Photographers combine physical image capture with post‑production editing. AI tools can automate many editing and compositional tasks, reducing demand for manual work, but the core shooting activity remains hands‑on and location‑dependent, limiting full digital replacement.

name: Video editors
ai_rationale: Video editing is a fully digital workflow; AI can automate cuts, color grading, effects, and even generate footage, dramatically boosting productivity. While creative judgment remains, generative and assistive tools already handle core tasks, placing the role in the high‑exposure bracket.

name: Animation / VFX
ai_rationale: Animation and VFX are fully digital, relying on software for modeling, rendering, and compositing; generative AI can already produce assets, automate rotoscoping, and speed up rendering pipelines, dramatically reshaping productivity. While artistic direction still needs human judgment, the core production tasks are highly automatable, giving the role a strong AI exposure.

name: Translators
hiring_trend_label: Strongly declining (>10% YoY drop in headcount)
ai_rationale: Translation is a largely digital task; LLMs and neural MT now achieve near‑human quality for most Indian language pairs, handling bulk work and displacing volume translators. AI dramatically boosts productivity, eroding demand for standard translators, especially given India's export‑oriented language services. Specialized post‑editing remains, but core translation is highly exposed.

name: Journalists
ai_rationale: Journalism is a predominantly digital knowledge job where AI can already draft articles, summarize reports, and generate routine news content, reducing the need for human writers. The declining hiring trend and export‑oriented Indian media services amplify exposure, though editorial judgment and investigative reporting remain human‑centric.

name: Lawyers (litigation)
ai_rationale: Litigation lawyers perform a mix of digital tasks (legal research, drafting pleadings, document review) that AI can substantially augment, while courtroom advocacy and strategic judgment remain human‑centric. In India, a large export‑oriented legal services segment makes these digital components highly exposed, though regulatory and professional norms protect the advocacy element.

name: HR professionals
ai_rationale: HR work is largely digital—resume databases, payroll systems, analytics and internal communications—so AI can automate screening, routine queries and data processing, boosting productivity. However, core functions like employee relations, negotiation and strategic planning require human judgment and interpersonal skills, limiting full replacement.

name: Paralegals & legal research
ai_rationale: Paralegal work is largely digital—researching statutes, drafting briefs, reviewing contracts—tasks that LLMs already perform at near‑human quality, especially in India’s export‑oriented legal BPO sector. AI can dramatically boost productivity, reducing demand for junior staff, while regulatory or client preferences may slow full replacement.

name: Corporate legal counsel
ai_rationale: Work is largely digital (contract drafting, legal research, document review) so AI can automate many routine tasks and boost productivity, but strategic judgment, client interaction, and regulatory nuance remain human‑centric. Indian legal outsourcing amplifies exposure, yet the senior advisory role retains defensible human elements.

name: Management consultants
ai_rationale: Consultants produce digital analyses, presentations, and strategic recommendations that AI can generate, summarize, and model, dramatically boosting productivity and reducing demand for junior consultants. The work is export‑oriented and highly digital, making it vulnerable to AI‑driven automation despite current growth.

name: Truck drivers
ai_rationale: Truck driving is primarily physical and requires real‑time navigation and vehicle control in varied Indian road conditions. While autonomous driving research exists, limited infrastructure, regulatory barriers, and low cost advantage keep AI impact modest, mainly as driver‑assist tools rather than replacement.

name: Taxi & cab drivers
ai_rationale: Driving is a physical, real‑time task that relies on on‑road judgment and dexterity; AI‑driven autonomous vehicles are not yet viable in India's infrastructure and cost constraints. AI can assist with navigation and dispatch, but cannot replace the driver, so exposure remains low.

name: Last-mile delivery (non-platform / courier)
ai_rationale: Work is primarily physical—picking, transporting, and delivering parcels—requiring on‑ground navigation and hand‑off. AI can improve routing and demand forecasting, but autonomous delivery is limited in India, so core tasks remain human. Hence exposure is low to moderate.

name: Warehouse workers
ai_rationale: Warehouse work is primarily physical—picking, packing, loading—requiring manual dexterity and on‑site presence. AI‑driven robotics and inventory software could augment tasks, but high capital costs and limited adoption in India keep automation impact modest. Hence exposure is low to moderate.

name: Logistics coordinators
ai_rationale: Logistics coordinators work largely with digital scheduling, routing, and communication platforms, making their tasks highly automatable by AI-driven optimization and chat‑bot interfaces. In India, many such roles are export‑oriented back‑office services, so AI productivity gains can sharply reduce demand. Physical presence is minimal, but some human judgment on exceptions remains.

name: Pilots & airline crew
ai_rationale: Piloting and cabin crew rely on real-time physical control, situational judgment, and passenger interaction, which AI cannot fully replicate. While AI‑driven decision‑support and autopilot systems augment tasks, the core role remains manual and safety‑critical, limiting automation potential.

name: Restaurant staff
ai_rationale: Work is largely physical and interpersonal—taking orders, serving food, cleaning—so AI can only assist (e.g., digital ordering kiosks, kitchen management software). The core tasks require real‑time human judgment and dexterity, limiting automation potential despite some digital tools.

name: Hotel staff
ai_rationale: Hotel staff perform primarily in‑person service (check‑in, housekeeping, guest assistance) that relies on physical presence and interpersonal judgment. AI can support back‑office tasks (booking systems, chatbots) but cannot replace the core front‑line duties, yielding low exposure.

name: Event management
ai_rationale: Event management relies on in‑person coordination, client interaction, and on‑site logistics, with limited digital product creation. AI can assist with scheduling, budgeting, and marketing analytics, but core tasks require human judgment and physical presence, keeping exposure low.

name: Tour guides
ai_rationale: Tour guiding is a highly interpersonal, on‑site role requiring real‑time cultural interpretation and physical presence; AI can augment with audio apps or translation tools but cannot replace the human guide’s situational judgment and engagement. Low digital work product keeps exposure modest.

name: Travel agents
ai_rationale: Core tasks—searching, comparing, and booking travel services—are largely digital and already handled by AI‑driven platforms and chatbots, reducing the need for human agents. Indian travel agencies face export‑type competition and declining hiring, indicating strong productivity displacement despite some regulatory or relationship‑based protection.

name: Plantation workers (tea, coffee, rubber)
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Plantation work is manual, outdoor labor requiring physical dexterity and real‑time judgment of plant health, tasks that AI cannot digitize or automate at scale in India. Limited digital interfaces and low wage arbitrage mean AI offers minimal productivity gains.

name: Horticulture & orchard workers
ai_rationale: Horticulture and orchard work is primarily manual, outdoor labor involving planting, pruning, harvesting, and pest control, with minimal digital output. AI tools for precision agriculture exist but are not widely deployed at the worker level in India, and the tasks require physical dexterity and real‑time judgment, limiting automation potential.

name: Forestry & timber workers
ai_rationale: Work is primarily manual, outdoors, involving tree felling, planting, and timber transport, with minimal digital interaction. AI tools have limited applicability to core tasks, and infrastructure constraints reduce automation potential.

name: Veterinarians & livestock specialists
ai_rationale: Veterinary work is heavily hands‑on, requiring physical examination, surgery, and animal handling; AI can aid diagnostics and record‑keeping but cannot replace the tactile and judgment aspects. Limited digital product means modest automation potential, yielding a low‑moderate exposure score.

name: Beekeepers & sericulture workers
ai_rationale: Beekeeping and sericulture involve hands‑on animal care, hive/​silk‑worm management, and field work that require tactile skill and real‑time judgment in uncontrolled environments. AI tools can aid data logging or disease detection, but core tasks remain physical and low‑digital, limiting automation potential.

name: Agricultural extension officers
ai_rationale: The role is largely field‑based, requiring on‑site farmer interaction, demonstrations, and local knowledge; AI can augment advice through decision‑support tools and remote diagnostics, but cannot replace the physical presence and trust‑based communication. Hence exposure is modest, fitting the 2‑3 bracket.

name: Masons (rajmistri)
ai_rationale: Masonry is a physically intensive, on-site trade with minimal digital work product; AI can assist design planning but cannot replace manual bricklaying or site work. Low digitality and high reliance on manual dexterity keep exposure near the bottom of the scale.

name: Carpenters
ai_rationale: Carpentry is a hands‑on, physical trade requiring manual dexterity, on‑site judgment, and minimal digital interaction. AI can assist design or planning tools, but core fabrication cannot be automated with current technology, especially given India's labor cost advantage and limited robotics adoption in construction.

name: Electricians (construction & wiring)
ai_rationale: Work is hands‑on wiring, installation, and troubleshooting in unpredictable construction sites, requiring physical dexterity and on‑site judgment. AI can aid design planning or diagnostics, but cannot replace the manual tasks, and Indian infrastructure limits robotics adoption.

name: Plumbers

name: Private security guards
ai_rationale: The role is primarily physical, requiring on‑site presence, patrolling and interaction with people, with minimal digital work. AI can augment surveillance (e.g., video analytics) but cannot replace the human judgment and deterrence functions, so exposure remains low.

name: Cash logistics & armed escort
ai_rationale: The role is physically intensive, involving cash handling, armored vehicle operation, and armed escort, tasks that require real‑time human judgment and physical presence. AI can aid route planning or surveillance analytics, but cannot replace the core security and dexterity functions, especially given limited automation in Indian security services.

name: Bouncers & event security
ai_rationale: Bouncers & event security rely on physical presence, real‑time judgment, and interpersonal deterrence, tasks that are not digitizable. AI can assist with background checks or video monitoring, but cannot replace on‑site human enforcement, especially in India's low‑wage, high‑touch security market.

name: Real estate brokers & agents
ai_rationale: The role is heavily based on personal networking, negotiations, and physical property viewings, with limited digital work product. AI can assist with lead generation, pricing analytics, and virtual tours, but cannot replace the trust‑based human interaction central to brokerage. Hence exposure is modest, around a 3.

name: Property valuers & surveyors
ai_rationale: Valuation and surveying combine physical site inspections with market data analysis; AI can automate data gathering, modeling, and report generation, reshaping a large portion of the work, but field measurements and judgment calls remain human-intensive. The role is not heavily export‑oriented, limiting wage‑arbitrage pressure, but digital augmentation is significant.

name: Property & facility managers
ai_rationale: Facility managers rely on on‑site inspections, vendor coordination, and physical problem‑solving, with limited digital output. AI can augment scheduling, IoT‑based maintenance alerts, and data analytics, reshaping parts of the role, but core judgment and physical presence remain essential, placing exposure at a moderate level.

name: Professional athletes & sportspersons
ai_rationale: Performance relies on physical skill, real‑time human judgment and audience appeal, which AI cannot replicate. The work is non‑digital and largely immune to automation, despite modest data analytics support in training.

name: Sports coaches & trainers
ai_rationale: Coaching relies on real‑time physical interaction, motivation, and nuanced judgment that AI cannot replicate; AI tools (video analytics, training apps) may augment but not replace the role. Low digital work product and limited offshore wage pressure keep exposure modest.

name: Umpires, referees & match officials
ai_rationale: Umpiring relies on real‑time physical observation and judgment in unpredictable sporting events, with limited digital work product. AI tools (e.g., video replay, VAR) assist but cannot replace on‑field decisions, so exposure remains low despite modest tech aids.

name: NGO & development sector staff
ai_rationale: NGO staff blend field outreach, stakeholder coordination, and report/grant writing. While parts of documentation and data analysis can be automated, much work requires on‑ground interaction and contextual judgment, limiting full digital substitution. India's large NGO sector and modest wage arbitrage keep the role relatively insulated, yielding a mid‑range exposure.
source: Bain India Philanthropy Report + Ministry of Home (FCRA)

name: Social workers (MSW / professional)
ai_rationale: Social work relies heavily on face‑to‑face counseling, empathy, and nuanced judgment in complex social contexts, with only modest paperwork that can be digitised. AI can assist with data entry or resource matching, but cannot replace the core interpersonal functions, especially in India's NGO and public‑sector settings.

name: Child welfare & protection workers
ai_rationale: The role is centered on in‑person assessments, counseling, and safeguarding children, requiring nuanced human judgment and trust. AI can aid paperwork and data tracking, but cannot replace the core relational and field‑based tasks, leading to low overall exposure.

name: Coal mine workers
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Work is physically intensive, underground, and requires manual dexterity and real‑time human judgment; AI tools can aid monitoring but cannot replace core tasks. Limited digitality and high capital constraints keep automation exposure low in India.
source: Ministry of Coal + Coal India Ltd annual report

name: Metal & iron ore miners
ai_rationale: Mining involves physical extraction, heavy equipment operation, and on‑site decision‑making in unpredictable environments, tasks that are not digit‑centric. AI can assist with predictive maintenance or exploration data, but core labor remains manual, limiting exposure.

name: Stone & limestone quarry workers
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Quarry work is physically intensive, requiring manual extraction, loading, and transport of stone with minimal digital interaction. AI tools have limited relevance to core tasks, and low wage arbitrage or export potential does not increase exposure.

name: Oil & gas field workers (upstream)
ai_rationale: Upstream oil & gas field work is primarily physical, involving equipment operation, maintenance, and on‑site troubleshooting in remote locations. Tasks are not digit‑centric, and AI tools can only assist with monitoring or data analysis, not replace the hands‑on labor. Indian infrastructure and wage dynamics further limit automation impact.

name: Petroleum refinery & petrochemical workers
ai_rationale: Petroleum refinery & petrochemical work centers on physical plant operation, equipment monitoring, maintenance, and safety tasks that require manual dexterity and on‑site presence. AI can support predictive maintenance and process optimization but cannot replace the hands‑on, real‑world interventions, keeping exposure low.
source: Petroleum Federation of India + IOCL/BPCL/HPCL filings

name: Mining engineers
ai_rationale: Mining engineering combines field supervision, equipment operation, and safety-critical decisions with some CAD and simulation work. AI can augment design and autonomous drilling, but the role remains heavily physical and site‑based, limiting full digital replacement in the near term.

name: Geologists & exploration scientists
ai_rationale: Geologists rely heavily on digital data (seismic, GIS, modeling) where AI can automate interpretation and accelerate analysis, boosting productivity. However, field sampling, on‑site judgment, and regulatory approvals require human expertise, limiting full replacement. In India, the role is not export‑driven and wages are modest, so AI‑driven productivity gains are the main exposure.

name: Petroleum engineers
ai_rationale: Petroleum engineering relies heavily on digital simulation, data analysis, and modeling, which AI can augment and accelerate, potentially reducing the number of engineers needed for routine reservoir studies. However, field decision‑making, on‑site supervision, and complex problem‑solving remain human‑centric, limiting full replacement.
source: DGH + Society of Petroleum Engineers India

name: Power plant operators
ai_rationale: Operators monitor and control plant equipment via SCADA systems; AI can augment monitoring and predictive maintenance but core safety decisions and real‑time human judgment remain essential. Digital tasks are present but physical oversight and regulatory constraints limit full automation, placing exposure in the mixed physical/cognitive range.
source: Central Electricity Authority + NTPC/state genco filings

name: Electricity distribution (DISCOM) staff
ai_rationale: DISCOM staff perform largely physical tasks—meter reading, line inspection, fault repair—and rely on on‑site judgment. AI can aid scheduling, outage prediction, and remote monitoring, but core work remains hands‑on and low‑digital, limiting exposure to moderate levels.

name: Water utility & treatment workers
ai_rationale: Work involves physical operation and maintenance of water treatment plants, field inspections, and manual valve control, tasks that require on‑site presence and dexterity. AI can assist with monitoring and predictive maintenance but cannot replace the hands‑on labor. Hence exposure is low, around the lower end of the scale.
source: Ministry of Jal Shakti + Central Public Health Engineering

name: Sanitation & sewage workers
ai_rationale: Sanitation and sewage work is primarily physical, outdoors, and requires manual dexterity in unpredictable environments, leaving little room for AI to perform core tasks. AI may support monitoring or route optimization but does not replace the hands‑on labor involved. In India, low wages and the informal nature of the work further limit automation adoption.

name: Gas pipeline & CGD workers
ai_rationale: Work is largely physical—installing, inspecting, and maintaining gas pipelines and CGD infrastructure—with limited digital output. AI can aid monitoring and diagnostics, but core tasks require on‑site dexterity and safety judgment, keeping exposure low.

name: Electrical lineworkers (utility)
ai_rationale: Electrical linework is a physically demanding, outdoor job requiring manual dexterity, real‑time safety judgments, and minimal digital interaction. AI can aid planning or inspection via drones, but core tasks remain non‑digital and hard to automate, especially given India's capital constraints in utilities.

name: Energy engineers (utilities)
ai_rationale: Energy engineers rely heavily on simulation, data analytics, and optimization software, all of which are increasingly AI‑augmented, making core tasks highly digital. While field inspections and regulatory compliance require physical presence, AI can boost productivity enough to reshape hiring demand, especially given India's export‑oriented engineering services.

name: Utility billing & meter readers
ai_rationale: Meter reading is increasingly replaceable by smart meters and AI‑driven remote data capture, while billing is a digital process that AI can automate and accelerate. The role’s core tasks are largely data‑centric and digital, making it highly vulnerable to AI‑driven productivity gains, though some on‑site verification may persist.

name: Telecom tower & site technicians
ai_rationale: The role is primarily physical, involving installation, maintenance, and troubleshooting of telecom towers, which requires on‑site dexterity and judgment. AI can assist with predictive maintenance analytics, but core tasks remain hands‑on and location‑bound, limiting automation potential.

name: Telecom network engineers
ai_rationale: Network engineers handle largely digital tasks—network design, configuration, monitoring—where AI-driven automation and analytics can boost productivity and reduce staffing needs. However, significant on‑site hardware installation and troubleshooting remain, limiting full replacement. In India, growing telecom demand sustains hiring despite AI gains.

name: Telecom field sales & retention
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: The role blends physical, in‑person sales visits with data‑driven retention tasks; AI can automate prospect scoring, churn prediction, and digital upselling, reducing the need for many field agents. However, relationship building and on‑site service still require human presence, limiting full digital replacement.

name: Optical fibre installers (FTTH)
ai_rationale: The role is primarily physical, requiring manual dexterity to lay and splice fibre in diverse field conditions. AI can aid planning, route optimization, and provide AR guidance, but cannot perform the hands‑on installation, keeping exposure low (2).

name: RF / Spectrum engineers
ai_rationale: RF/Spectrum engineering is a highly digital knowledge role involving simulation, data analysis, and regulatory planning, which AI can automate and accelerate. AI-driven optimization tools can markedly boost productivity, potentially reducing demand, though human expertise for complex spectrum policy and on‑site troubleshooting remains essential.

name: Broadcast & cable engineers
hiring_trend_label: Slowly declining (<3% YoY drop or stagnant)
ai_rationale: Broadcast & cable engineers handle RF hardware, transmission networks, and field installations, tasks that are partly digital (monitoring, configuration) but also require physical setup and real‑time troubleshooting. AI can automate diagnostics, optimize spectrum use, and aid design, reshaping parts of the role, yet core on‑site work remains human‑centric.

name: Submarine & undersea cable operations
ai_rationale: The role combines physical field work (cable laying, repairs, ship operations) with network monitoring. AI can augment monitoring and fault prediction, but core tasks require hands‑on expertise and cannot be digitized, keeping exposure modest.

name: Solar PV installers & technicians
ai_rationale: Work is primarily physical installation and maintenance of solar panels on site, requiring manual dexterity and on‑site judgment. AI can aid design, site analysis, and predictive maintenance, but cannot replace the hands‑on tasks, keeping exposure low despite modest digital assistance.
source: MNRE + Skill Council for Green Jobs

name: Wind turbine technicians
ai_rationale: Wind turbine technicians perform on‑site maintenance, inspections, and repairs that require physical dexterity, safety judgments, and real‑time problem solving in harsh environments. AI can aid diagnostics and training, but core tasks remain non‑digital and hands‑on, limiting automation potential in the near term.

name: EV battery manufacturing workers
ai_rationale: The role is primarily physical assembly and quality checks in battery plants, with limited digital work product. AI can aid process monitoring and predictive maintenance, but core manual tasks and dexterity requirements keep automation potential modest. Indian labor cost advantage and capital constraints further limit rapid AI-driven replacement.
source: ARAI + Society of Manufacturers of Electric Vehicles

name: EV charging station operators
ai_rationale: The job is primarily physical, requiring on‑site presence to manage hardware, handle cash/card payments and perform basic troubleshooting. AI can augment monitoring and remote diagnostics, but cannot replace the manual interaction with charging equipment, so exposure remains modest.
source: Power Ministry EV-charging guidelines + state DISCOMs

name: Solar PV engineers & designers
ai_rationale: The role relies heavily on digital design tools and simulation, which AI can augment or partially automate, especially for layout optimization and performance modeling. However, substantial on‑site engineering, site assessment, and installation oversight remain physical and judgment‑heavy, limiting full replacement.
source: MNRE + Skill Council for Green Jobs

name: Sustainability & ESG analysts
ai_rationale: ESG analysts work primarily with digital data, regulatory filings, and produce written assessments, all of which AI can extract, analyze, and draft at scale. Large language models already generate ESG reports and score companies, reducing demand for junior analysts, especially in export‑oriented Indian research firms. Human judgment on nuanced materiality and stakeholder engagement remains a barrier, keeping the role partially defensible.

name: Green hydrogen technologists
ai_rationale: Green hydrogen technologists work on process design, simulation, and plant optimization, which are highly digital tasks. AI can significantly boost modeling, predictive maintenance, and data analysis, reducing labor demand, but physical plant work and regulatory expertise remain essential.
